title: gnidia tenella var. elatior
intro: Gnidia tenella var. elatior, also known as the "Horse's Tear Plant," is a shrub-like plant species native to Africa, characterized by its small, white flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gnidia tenella var. elatior include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and treating infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is also applied topically as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions.
